frameworks.name: Fisher information
frameworks.one_line: Quantifies how precisely a stimulus can be estimated from neural activity.

frameworks.name: mutual information / information theory
frameworks.one_line: Information-theoretic measure linking neural activity and stimulus.

frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: Stimulus represented by activity of a large array of tuned neurons.

frameworks.name: parameter estimation and model selection
frameworks.one_line: Setting in which the Fisher-information link was originally exhibited.

core_question: How is the mutual information between a population's activity and a tuned stimulus related to Fisher information, and what does this imply for optimizing tuning curves?
key_assumptions: Large array of neurons tuned to the stimulus
Stimulus represented by an angular variable
key_findings: A direct link between Fisher information and information-theoretic quantities can be given a standard information-theoretic interpretation
For population coding, mutual information between population activity and stimulus is naturally related to Fisher information
interpretations: This relation provides a principled basis for optimizing tuning-curve parameters in neurons coding an angular stimulus
